hypothesis: If NEB is executed on a pretrained EquiformerV2 surrogate with Bayesian Algorithm Execution that iteratively acquires informative ground-truth labels from path samples, then LJ7 and LJ38 minimum-energy paths will reach the same fmax convergence with low MAEi using significantly fewer expensive simulator evaluations than classical NEB, because uncertainty-guided fine-tuning efficiently approximates the relevant potential-energy surface.
